title: CEO Commentary on Shares & Deposits: As of March 31, 2026
content:
Total member shares increased by $498 as of March 31, 2026, representing a 0.2% gain. This growth reflects continued stability in the funding base.

Share certificates remain the largest funding component at 41.8% of total shares, totaling $83,702 thousand ($83.7 million), while share drafts account for $26,719 thousand (13.3%), continuing to represent a meaningful portion of core deposits. Regular shares total $38,295 thousand (19.1%) of the funding mix, while nonmember deposits represent $30,255 thousand (15.1%). Smaller balances within other share categories remain limited.

Most core deposit categories grew from the previous period, particularly money market shares by 7.8%, although these balances remain immaterial to the overall funding structure -- include the immateriality phrase only if that category's absolute size is genuinely small.
